original: Despite a lack of interest in staff-based weaponry from most militaries, Victor Steele personally oversaw the design and release of the Steelestaff. It was one of the last products he would have the opportunity to personally spend time on. Perhaps motivated by his own growing infirmities, the Steelestaff was designed to be as much a tool as a weapon. Its hollowed interior allows it to function as a walking stick for the infirm, yet the precisely manufactured interior lattice allows it to maintain the strength of a weapon solidly constructed from its metallic namesake. Better still, the Steelestaff comes complete with a wide-array of survival tools and technology: waterproof storage compartments, deployable shovel blades at the bottom, emergency locator beacons, a combination electro-shocker & jump-starter, perpetual kinetic-recharge systems, a camp illumination mode, and more.\n\nYou can still remember his voice, promising that one day you’ll be glad such a thing exists. Is today that day?
